epic.name: Incident Lifecycle & Slack Bidirectional Sync
epic.objective: Complete the incident lifecycle with Slack bidirectional sync: interactive button handlers, slash commands, message-to-timeline sync, and frontend create incident modal
epic.definition_of_done: Users can acknowledge/resolve incidents from Slack buttons, create incidents via /incident slash command, Slack channel messages appear in incident timeline, and incidents can be created from the web UI modal
epic.notes: Status workflow, timeline entries, incident detail page, and one-way Slack posting are already implemented in v0.1. This epic focuses on what's missing: bidirectional Slack sync and the create incident modal.

tasks.name: Implement Slack Socket Mode listener
tasks.description: Set up Slack Socket Mode connection to receive interactive events (button clicks, slash commands) without exposing a public URL. Socket Mode uses WebSocket via SLACK_APP_TOKEN.
acceptance_criteria: Socket Mode connection established using slack-go socketmode package
Connection authenticated via SLACK_APP_TOKEN (xapp-...)
Automatic reconnection on disconnect with exponential backoff
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
Event dispatch loop running in background goroutine
Logs connection status (connected, disconnected, reconnecting)
Application starts without Socket Mode if SLACK_APP_TOKEN not set (degraded mode)
tasks.implementation_notes: Socket Mode is preferred over Events API for self-hosted apps because it doesn't require a public URL or SSL certificate. Uses WebSocket instead of HTTP webhooks.

tasks.name: Implement Slack interactive button handlers
tasks.description: Handle Acknowledge and Resolve button clicks from Slack incident messages. When a user clicks a button in the incident channel, update the incident status and post a confirmation message.
acceptance_criteria: InteractionCallback events dispatched from Socket Mode listener
Acknowledge button: transitions incident to 'acknowledged' status
Resolve button: transitions incident to 'resolved' status
Status transition validated (same state machine as API)
Incident updated in database with AcknowledgedAt/ResolvedAt timestamps
Timeline entry created: type=status_changed, actor_type=slack_user, actor_id=slack_user_id
Original Slack message updated to reflect new status (emoji, fields)
Confirmation message posted to channel: '@user acknowledged INC-042'
Invalid transitions return ephemeral error to user (e.g., already resolved)
Button click from non-incident channel handled gracefully
tasks.implementation_notes: Reuse existing incident_service.UpdateIncident() for status transitions. Map Slack user ID to actor for timeline entries.

tasks.name: Implement /incident slash command handler
tasks.description: Handle /incident slash command in Slack for creating and managing incidents directly from any Slack channel
acceptance_criteria: SlashCommand events dispatched from Socket Mode listener
/incident new [title] - opens modal dialog for incident creation
Modal includes fields: title (required), severity (dropdown), summary (optional)
On submit: creates incident via incident_service.CreateIncident()
Slack channel auto-created for new incident (existing flow)
Response posted to originating channel with incident link
Timeline entry: type=incident_created, actor_type=slack_user
/incident list - shows last 5 open incidents as ephemeral message
/incident help - shows usage instructions as ephemeral message
Invalid subcommand returns help text
tasks.implementation_notes: Uses Slack modal views (views.open API). Modal submission is a separate event type (view_submission) handled in the Socket Mode listener.

tasks.name: Implement Slack message-to-timeline sync
tasks.description: Capture messages posted in incident Slack channels and create corresponding timeline entries, providing a complete incident record in the web UI
acceptance_criteria: Subscribe to message events in incident channels via Socket Mode
New messages in incident channels create timeline entries: type=slack_message
Timeline entry content includes: message text, author name, author avatar URL, timestamp
Bot messages and system messages are excluded (no echo loops)
Thread replies captured with parent message reference
Message edits update the corresponding timeline entry content
Message deletions add a timeline entry: type=slack_message_deleted
Rate limited: max 1 timeline entry per second per channel to prevent flooding
Only channels matching inc-* pattern are monitored
tasks.implementation_notes: Filter messages by channel name prefix 'inc-'. Look up incident by slack_channel_id in the database. Ignore messages from the bot itself to prevent loops.

tasks.name: Update Slack message on incident status change from API/UI
tasks.description: When incident status changes via API or web UI, update the original Slack message to reflect the new status
acceptance_criteria: Status change via PATCH /api/v1/incidents/:id triggers Slack message update
Original incident message updated with new status, emoji, and timestamp
Action buttons updated: hide Acknowledge if acknowledged, hide both if resolved
Status update message posted to channel: 'Incident status changed to {status} by {actor}'
Works for all transitions: triggered→acknowledged, triggered→resolved, acknowledged→resolved
No Slack update if incident has no slack_channel_id (graceful skip)
Stores message_ts from initial message for future updates
tasks.implementation_notes: Partially implemented - PostStatusUpdateToSlack() exists but doesn't update the original message or modify buttons. Need to store initial message_ts and use UpdateMessage().

tasks.name: Implement create incident modal in frontend
tasks.description: Build the 'Declare Incident' modal dialog in the React frontend, replacing the TODO placeholder in IncidentsListPage and HomePage
acceptance_criteria: Modal triggered by 'Declare incident' button (replaces console.log TODO)
Form fields: Title (required, text input), Severity (required, dropdown: critical/high/medium/low), Summary (optional, textarea)
Form validation: title required and min 5 chars, severity required
Submit calls POST /api/v1/incidents with CreateIncidentRequest
Loading state shown during submission
On success: close modal, navigate to new incident detail page, show success toast
On error: show error message inline in modal, keep form state
Escape key and backdrop click close modal (with unsaved changes warning)
Accessible: focus trap, aria labels, keyboard navigation
tasks.implementation_notes: API endpoint POST /api/v1/incidents already exists. Frontend API client createIncident() already exists. Just need the modal UI component.

tasks.name: Add Slack channel link to incident detail page
tasks.description: Display the Slack channel link in the incident detail page properties panel so users can quickly jump to the Slack channel
acceptance_criteria: Slack channel name displayed in PropertiesPanel with Slack icon
Channel name is a clickable link opening Slack channel in new tab
Link format: slack://channel?team={team_id}&id={channel_id} for desktop app
Fallback web URL: https://app.slack.com/client/{team_id}/{channel_id}
Shows 'No channel' or 'Slack not configured' when slack_channel_id is empty
Channel name prefixed with # symbol for visual consistency
tasks.implementation_notes: Data already available in incident API response (slack_channel_id, slack_channel_name). Just need UI display in PropertiesPanel component.

tasks.name: Implement incident channel archival on resolution
tasks.description: When an incident is resolved, automatically archive the Slack channel after a configurable delay to keep the workspace clean
acceptance_criteria: When incident status changes to 'resolved', schedule channel archival
Final message posted before archival: 'This incident has been resolved. Channel will be archived.'
Configurable delay before archival (default: 24 hours) via SLACK_ARCHIVE_DELAY env var
Timeline entry: type=slack_channel_archived
Archival failure logged but does not affect incident status
Re-opening incident (resolved→acknowledged) cancels pending archival
Channel remains accessible (read-only) after archival
tasks.implementation_notes: Use Redis delayed job or goroutine with timer. ArchiveChannel() already implemented in ChatService. For v0.2, simple goroutine with sleep is acceptable; Redis-backed scheduling in v0.3+.

tasks.name: Add Slack integration tests
tasks.description: Write integration tests for the bidirectional Slack sync features using mock Slack API
acceptance_criteria: Mock Socket Mode client for testing without real Slack
Test: button click → incident status updated
Test: slash command → incident created
Test: message in incident channel → timeline entry created
Test: status change via API → Slack message updated
Test: invalid button click → ephemeral error returned
Test: message from bot → ignored (no echo loop)
Test: channel archival on resolution
All tests pass in CI without Slack credentials
tasks.implementation_notes: Create MockChatService and MockSocketModeClient for testing. Tests should not require actual Slack API access.

tasks.name: Update documentation for v0.2 Slack features
tasks.description: Update README, .env.example, and Slack app setup guide for new bidirectional features
acceptance_criteria: README updated with v0.2 features (bidirectional sync, slash commands)
.env.example updated with SLACK_APP_TOKEN and SLACK_ARCHIVE_DELAY
Slack app setup guide: additional scopes needed (commands, im:history, channels:history)
Slack app setup guide: enable Socket Mode in app settings
Slack app setup guide: add slash command /incident
Slack app setup guide: subscribe to message events
Troubleshooting section for Socket Mode connection issues
tasks.implementation_notes: SLACK_APP_TOKEN already in .env.example but documented as optional for Socket Mode. Now it becomes required for bidirectional sync.

external: Slack app must have Socket Mode enabled
Additional Slack scopes: commands, channels:history, users:read
